rawCommits.subject: Fix janky chat scroll at bottom during sidekick resize
rawCommits.body:
Four systems were competing to keep the chat pinned to the bottom while
dragging the sidekick divider: a custom per-message ResizeObserver, a
dedicated streaming-bubble ResizeObserver, a totalSize-keyed layout
effect, and a whole resize-session context plumbed through DesktopShell
into useScrollAnchorV2 plus a settled-effect rAF. The first two fired
scrollTop corrections synchronously inside their observer callbacks
while the virtualizer wrapper still had its pre-commit (stale) totalSize
style, so scrollTop = scrollHeight landed at the old bottom and then
snapped forward when React committed. Reading-mode anchor restoration
used each message's own rect, so it remained visually stable - which is
why only the pinned-at-bottom path looked janky.

Collapse to a single correction path. The virtualizer's own
measureElement drives totalSize; a single useLayoutEffect keyed on
totalSize and the streaming fields runs one post-commit, pre-paint
correction with a fresh scrollHeight. The per-message ResizeObserver is
kept solely to keep the message height cache warm for rows that later
scroll out of the active window - it no longer fires any scroll
correction. Remove the entire ChatResizeSessionContext plumbing,
DesktopShell's onResizeStart wiring, the streaming-bubble observer, and
the settled-effect remeasure pass.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Fix janky chat scroll at bottom during sidekick resize

rawCommits.subject: Pin chat to bottom synchronously during lane resize
rawCommits.body:
The prior refactor consolidated our corrections to a single post-commit
useLayoutEffect on totalSize, but that still leaves a one-frame lag
between the DOM rewrap during a sidekick drag and the React commit that
applies the virtualizer's new totalSize - so when pinned at the bottom,
each drag frame paints with stale translateY values and a stale
scrollHeight, which is the visible "bottom pops around" jank.

TanStack Virtual already has a synchronous hook for exactly this case:
`shouldAdjustScrollPositionOnItemSizeChange`, invoked from inside the
virtualizer's own ResizeObserver callback (pre-paint, same frame as the
layout change). Its default only returns true for items above the
viewport (anchor preservation while reading), which is why the pinned
path looked stable while scrolled up and janky while pinned at bottom.

Hook our `isAutoFollowing` state into it so every item size change
during follow mode accumulates into `scrollAdjustments` and scrollTop
is adjusted by the same delta on the same frame, keeping the visible
bottom pinned. Reading mode keeps the library's default behavior.

ChatMessageList now takes an `isAutoFollowing` prop wired from
ChatPanel (mirrored into a ref so the callback reads the latest
value). The post-commit totalSize/streaming effect is retained as a
safety re-sync but is a no-op in the steady pinned case.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Pin chat to bottom synchronously during lane resize

rawCommits.subject: Stop scrollTop clamp from swallowing pin-to-bottom delta on resize
rawCommits.body:
The previous commit wired `shouldAdjustScrollPositionOnItemSizeChange`
so the virtualizer would try to adjust scrollTop for every visible
item's size change during a sidekick drag. That part works, but the
visible pop was still happening because `resizeItem` calls
`scrollToFn(offset + delta)` *before* `itemSizeCache` is updated and
*well before* React commits the new `totalSize` style on the wrapper
div. So the browser clamped `scrollTop` against the stale
`maxScrollTop = scrollHeight_old - clientHeight`, which swallowed any
adjustment past the last item's delta. The "jump to the real bottom"
then happened on the next frame when React finally committed and our
`useLayoutEffect` wrote `scrollTop = scrollHeight`. With a fixed-height
tool card (spec/file preview) near the tail this was especially
visible: nearly all the delta came from middle prose re-wrapping, the
tail card contributed ~0, so the clamp ate almost the entire
adjustment.

Remove the clamp from the path. Take a ref on the virtualizer wrapper
div (the `position: relative; height: totalSize` one) and, in the
`shouldAdjustScrollPositionOnItemSizeChange` callback, bump
`wrapper.style.height` by the incoming delta and force a layout flush
(`void wrapper.offsetHeight`). That updates `scrollHeight` in the
same frame, so the virtualizer's following `scrollTo` lands at the
true new bottom without being clamped. React's next commit sets
`style.height = totalSize_new`, which matches what we already wrote,
so the reconciler is a no-op. A side benefit: the `scroll` event that
fires when the adjustment actually takes effect now reads a fresh
`scrollHeight` inside our `handleScroll`, so large drags can't flip
`pinnedRef` to false mid-drag on a stale `distFromBottom`.

Only grow, never shrink: when items shrink, the virtualizer's
`scrollTo(target)` target is already below the old max so no clamp
fires; the wrapper naturally reconciles back down on React's next
commit.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Stop scrollTop clamp from swallowing pin-to-bottom delta on resize

rawCommits.subject: Double chat textarea height and move scrollbar to row's right edge
rawCommits.body:
Raise the textarea's max-height from 200px to 400px and relocate the
vertical scrollbar from the textarea itself onto the input row, so it
renders past the send button instead of between the text and the
button. Attach/send buttons use sticky positioning to stay anchored
while the row scrolls.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Double chat textarea height and move scrollbar to row's right edge

rawCommits.subject: Let chat input grow with the viewport
rawCommits.body:
Replace the 400px cap on the chat textarea and its scrolling row with
min(70vh, 800px), and compute the matching JS ceiling from
window.innerHeight * 0.7 (capped at 800). A window-resize listener
re-runs autoResizeTextarea so the height stays correct when the
viewport changes, letting the input use the available lane height
instead of stopping short.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Let chat input grow with the viewport

rawCommits.subject: Unify chat lane artifacts behind a single Block primitive
rawCommits.body:
Every non-prose thing the agent emits (thinking, file ops, commands, specs,
tasks, list results, generated media, status readouts, unknown tools) now
renders through one shared `Block` shell: header with title and badge, a
fixed-height internal-scroll body, and a collapsible chevron. Tool rendering
is dispatched via a small `renderToolBlock` registry, replacing the big
per-tool if/else ladder in `ToolRow`.

Fixed body height is the point: once a Block is mounted it no longer grows
while content streams in, so the chat lane above and below stays stable and
live previews feel like a rolling `tail -f` inside the block instead of a
layout earthquake outside it.

Removed the bespoke `ThinkingRow`, `FilePreviewCard`, `SpecPreviewCard`,
`CommandPreviewCard`, `TaskCreatedIndicator`, and `SuperAgentToolCards`
components in favor of focused renderers under `components/Block/renderers/`.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Unify chat lane artifacts behind a single Block primitive

rawCommits.subject: Match feedback comment input to LLM chat input
rawCommits.body:
Replace the pill-styled comment field with a flush 32px row: send
button moves inside the textarea row, outer padding is gone, and only
a 1px top border separates it from the comment list. The input is now
an auto-resizing textarea and the send icon is ArrowUp, mirroring the
ChatInputBar primitives.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Match feedback comment input to LLM chat input

rawCommits.subject: Add Feedback app (phase 1, mock-backed)
rawCommits.body:
Introduces a new top-level Feedback app that mirrors the Feed/Profile
shell pattern. Left panel has grouped single-select filters for
Trending (sort), Type (category), and Status, each driving a local
Zustand store backed by mock data. Middle panel renders Feed-sized
cards with inline vote controls, a muted header line (author, time,
category), a colored status tag on the right, and a bottom-left
"N comment(s)" button. Right panel matches the Feed comments layout.

Post creation moved to a + button on the sidebar search action that
opens a ZUI Modal styled to match NewProjectModal / AddTaskForm, using
the shared Select component for category/status.

Also fixes a latent bug in resolveActiveApp and mobileNavigation where
pathname.startsWith('/feed') was swallowing '/feedback' into the Feed
app. Both now require a strict basePath match and have regression tests.

The requirements doc is updated to move vote storage to aura-network
(matching the Phase 3 plan) rather than Aura OS local persistence.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Add Feedback app (phase 1, mock-backed)

rawCommits.subject: Share scroll + folder-section chrome across Feed and Feedback
rawCommits.body:
Extract the Projects left-menu section header into a reusable
FolderSection component (label + rotating chevron + keyboard toggle +
optional suffix), and use it for the three Feedback left-panel groups
(Trending, Type, Status) in place of ad-hoc headings.

Also switch Feed and Feedback to the shared OverlayScrollbar used by
LeftMenuTree / ChatPanel / SidekickContent: main list, comments list,
and the Feedback filter panel now hide their native scrollbars and
render the overlay track as a sibling inside a position:relative shell.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Share scroll + folder-section chrome across Feed and Feedback

rawCommits.subject: Replace chat virtualization with native CSS scroll anchoring
rawCommits.body:
The virtualizer rendered each message with `position: absolute;
transform: translateY(start)` where `start` came from a cumulative sum
in the virtualizer's measurementsCache. React committed those translateY
values one frame after the ResizeObserver that caused them, so every
drag frame painted with stale positions before correcting, which the
user experienced as items sliding and popping during a sidekick lane
resize.

The last four commits tried to fix this from the JS side - running our
own anchor restoration via rAF-coalesced scroll writes, then forcing
the virtualizer's `shouldAdjustScrollPositionOnItemSizeChange` to true,
then imperatively bumping the wrapper's `style.height` to defeat
`scrollTop` clamping against a stale `scrollHeight`. Each step was
correct in isolation but none addressed the root: virtualization's
async-commit model is fundamentally at odds with synchronous scroll
adjustment inside a ResizeObserver batch, and the more JS we added
the more we fought the browser's own `overflow-anchor` mechanism,
which was being explicitly disabled in CSS *and* rendered moot by the
items' absolute positioning (abs-positioned elements are excluded from
scroll-anchor candidates by spec).

Switch to normal flex-column flow and let CSS scroll anchoring keep
the visible position stable. The browser compensates `scrollTop` for
any height change above the in-viewport anchor element within the same
layout pass, covering lane drags, streaming tokens that land in
messages above the viewport, tool-row expand/collapse, and
load-older-history prepends. The only case native anchoring doesn't
cover is growth *at* the anchor itself - the streaming bubble gaining
tokens, or a new message arriving while pinned - and for that we keep
a targeted `useLayoutEffect` that writes `scrollTop = scrollHeight`,
gated on `isAutoFollowing`.

Files:
- ChatMessageList: drop useVirtualizer + per-message ResizeObserver +
  measureElement plumbing + the wrapper-height hack + the
  totalSize-keyed pin effect. Render messages in a flex column; one
  small useLayoutEffect pins to bottom when the tail grows.
- ChatPanel.module.css: replace the two `overflow-anchor: none` rules
  on `.messageArea*` with `overflow-anchor: auto` so the browser can
  actually anchor.
- useScrollAnchorV2: drop captureAnchor, restoreAnchor,
  reconcileContent, onContentHeightChange, scrollToBottomIfPinned, the
  resize-session branches, and findFirstVisibleMessage. What's left is
  just handleScroll / scrollToBottom / isAutoFollowing plus the guard
  that keeps our own programmatic scroll writes from flipping the
  pinned state.
- useChatPanelState + ChatPanel.tsx: stop plumbing heightCache,
  captureAnchor, restoreAnchor, onContentHeightChange down into
  ChatMessageList.
- useLoadOlderMessages: drop captureAnchor / restoreAnchor arguments -
  the browser preserves the reading position automatically when
  content is prepended above the viewport.
- useMessageHeightCache: delete. No longer needed; the virtualizer was
  the only consumer.

Net -533 lines across 11 files. Tests updated to match the new surface;
33/33 chat tests pass. Lint count is below the pre-change baseline
because the old `any`-cast test fixtures were replaced with a typed
helper.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Replace chat virtualization with native CSS scroll anchoring

rawCommits.subject: Neutralize Block body borders and highlight colors
rawCommits.body:
Two chat-lane polish fixes, both scoped to the Block primitive so every
renderer inherits them automatically:

- Strip the outer-markdown pre border/padding that ancestor rulesets
  (`.root pre` in LLMOutput.module.css) were painting onto renderer
  content nested inside a Block body. Every Block already owns its shell
  border, so the SpecBlock no longer shows a second inner border.
- Collapse highlight.js colors inside a Block body onto the app's gray/
  white ramp: structural tokens (section headings, keywords, tags, bullets,
  strong/emphasis) use --color-text, everything else uses
  --color-text-secondary, comments use --color-text-muted. Spec and file
  previews now read in the same palette as the rest of the UI instead of
  the imported github-dark rainbow.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Neutralize Block body borders and highlight colors

rawCommits.subject: Align Block body text with header status dot
rawCommits.body:
Code, spec, and diff renderers use `flushBody` on the Block (the body
wrapper has no padding) so content can sit edge-to-edge with the scroll
viewport. That meant the previous 0-padding reset on the inner `pre` let
content slam against the left border instead of lining up with the
header's green status dot (10px from the block edge).

Move the 10px horizontal offset onto the `.codeArea` / `.diffArea`
wrappers themselves so code text, spec markdown, and diff lines all start
at the same x as the status dot and the title icon above them.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Align Block body text with header status dot

rawCommits.subject: Wire up feedback API end-to-end
rawCommits.body:
Backend:
- New `handlers/feedback.rs` with list / create / get / update-status /
  list-comments / add-comment / cast-vote endpoints, plus a router
  group in `router.rs` and the module entry in `handlers/mod.rs`.
- Matching integration-test file `tests/feedback_api_tests.rs`.
- Incidental rustfmt cleanups in `agents/chat.rs`, `spec_disk.rs`,
  `specs.rs`, `tests/api_tests.rs` (no behavior changes).

Frontend:
- New `interface/src/api/feedback.ts` client, surfaced via the root
  `api` object in `api/client.ts`.
- Feedback store rewritten to drive through the real API; mock-data
  module and its imports removed.
- `FeedbackMainPanel` and `NewFeedbackModal` adjusted to the
  API-backed store; store tests updated to match.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Add session delete and restyle sidekick sessions as task trees
rawCommits.body:
Right-click a session row in the Project sidekick Sessions tab or the
Agents app Chats tab to delete it. Sessions now render as an Explorer
tree with TaskStatusIcon suffixes so they visually match specs/tasks.

Backend wires a new DELETE /api/projects/:p/agents/:a/sessions/:s route
through to StorageClient.delete_session, with a mock-storage handler so
the integration test can exercise the full proxy path.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Add session delete and restyle sidekick sessions as task trees

rawCommits.subject: Scope session reset to drop prior-session LLM context
rawCommits.body:
Reset only cleared the in-memory caches and rotated the storage write
target — the cold-start rebuild still aggregated every past session, so a
corrupted tool_use block from a crashed harness kept getting re-injected
into the model on any cache miss / cold start / refresh. Split the read
path: UI history keeps aggregating across sessions while LLM-context
loaders now scope to the current storage session only. Also strip
dangling tool_use blocks (no matching tool_result) in both the
super-agent and harness conversion paths as a defensive guard against
future harness crashes.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Scope session reset to drop prior-session LLM context

rawCommits.subject: Collapse doubled borders between stacked tool call rows
rawCommits.body:
Adjacent Block borders in the activity timeline's tool group sat flush
with zero gap, so a row's top border stacked against the previous row's
bottom border and read as a 2px divider. Pull each subsequent row up by
-1px so neighboring borders overlap into a single clean 1px line.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Collapse doubled borders between stacked tool call rows

rawCommits.subject: Wire feedback votes and status updates through aura-network
rawCommits.body:
Replaces the phase-2 501 stubs: list/get now surface vote aggregates
inline, PATCH status forwards to aura-network's post metadata endpoint,
and POST vote round-trips through the new votes API. The UI applies
votes/status optimistically and reconciles with server aggregates,
reverting on failure.

rawCommits.subject: Flush TaskBlock body so collapsed rows match other tools
rawCommits.body:
TaskBlock was the only tool renderer not passing `flushBody`, so its
body retained the default 8px vertical padding and bled extra height
into the collapsed row. Switch to `flushBody` and move the indentation
into `.taskTitle` / `.taskDesc` so expanded task content keeps the same
inset while collapsed rows line up with the other tool blocks.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Flush TaskBlock body so collapsed rows match other tools

rawCommits.subject: Retire stale active sessions on rotation
rawCommits.body:
Sessions were being created with status=active and never transitioned
out of it, so every historical session rendered with the spinning
TaskStatusIcon in the sidekick. Before creating a new session in
resolve_chat_session (the path hit by both reset-session and fresh
chats) we now flip any prior active sessions for that agent instance
to completed with ended_at=now. Failures are logged and swallowed so
they never block new-session creation.

Also adds a small displaySessionStatus frontend guard so legacy active
rows already in storage render correctly: only the newest session per
group keeps its real status; older active rows collapse to completed.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Retire stale active sessions on rotation

rawCommits.subject: Surface upstream error code when project delete fails
rawCommits.body:
The aura-network backend returns a generic 500
{"error":{"code":"DATABASE","message":"An internal error occurred"}}
whenever a project can't be deleted due to a residual FK (agents,
sessions, feed entries, etc.). That bubbled up to the delete modal
as an opaque "An internal error occurred" with no hint of what to do.

Remap the specific DATABASE 500 on project delete to a 409 with an
actionable message pointing users at Archive, thread upstream codes
into ApiError.details for all network errors, and fall back to
appending the upstream code when the nested message is opaque.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Surface upstream error code when project delete fails

rawCommits.body:
Pin .blockHeader to a fixed --block-header-height and zero out the body
wrap's height and transparent border so every collapsed Block (Thought
and all tool renderers) matches the header row's rendered height.

rawCommits.subject: Tag feedback items with a product and filter by it
rawCommits.body:
Every feedback post is now tagged with a product (AURA, The Grid,
Wilder World, Z Chain) stored in the existing metadata JSON blob
alongside category and status. The Aura OS shell defaults to AURA; the
composer's product select follows the current Product filter so
switching to The Grid and posting from there tags the new item as The
Grid without an extra click.

The left filter panel gains a new "Product" FolderSection pinned above
Trending, collapsed by default with exactly one product selected at all
times. No DB migration — product piggy-backs on metadata via the same
pattern already used for feedbackCategory/feedbackStatus, with
server-side validation rejecting unknown values.

Legacy posts created before this landed default to AURA on read so they
remain visible while the shell's current product filter is AURA.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Block spec deletion when tasks still reference it
rawCommits.body:
Preflight list_tasks filtered by spec_id in delete_spec and return a
409 conflict with a clear message (mentioning the task count) instead
of silently proxying the delete. Upstream storage errors on the delete
now flow through map_storage_error so any storage-side 4xx is
preserved.

Replace the optimistic-delete-with-rollback flow in SpecList and
TaskList with a shared DeleteSpecModal + useDeleteSpec hook that shows
the server error inline via getApiErrorMessage, matching the existing
DeleteProjectModal pattern.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Stop feedback composer from stealing focus, pin composer to AURA
rawCommits.body:
ZUI's Modal includes `onClose` in its focus effect dep array and
re-runs `initialFocusRef.current.focus()` every time the callback
identity changes. Both the inline `handleClose` inside
NewFeedbackModal and the `() => setIsModalOpen(false)` we passed from
FeedbackList were fresh on every render, so every keystroke yanked
the cursor back to the title input. Memoize both callbacks so the
Modal's effect only fires on actual open/close.

Also drop the "composer follows the current Product filter" behaviour
in favour of a hard AURA default. The Product filter on the left and
the composer's Product select are now independent; switching the
filter to The Grid no longer retargets new posts.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Stop feedback composer from stealing focus, pin composer to AURA

rawCommits.cleanSubject: Size feedback and feed filter items to 32px

rawCommits.subject: Route feedback to its own aura-network client
rawCommits.body:
Add an optional feedback-scoped NetworkClient sourced from
AURA_NETWORK_FEEDBACK_URL so Feedback-app traffic can target a local
aura-network (which has the new vote and feedback-filter endpoints)
while every other handler keeps using the deployed AURA_NETWORK_URL.
When the override is unset, Feedback falls back to the main client so
the split cleans up automatically once prod aura-network adopts the
feedback endpoints.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Route feedback to its own aura-network client

rawCommits.subject: Add Notes app backed by local markdown files
rawCommits.body:
Introduces a new Notes app alongside Feed and Feedback that lets users
create and edit free-form notes organized by Aura project. Notes are
stored as markdown files under the data directory, with live first-line
titles, TipTap-powered WYSIWYG/markdown editing, and a sidekick that
surfaces file metadata, a generated table of contents, and local
comments stored in a sidecar JSON file.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Add Notes app backed by local markdown files

rawCommits.subject: Use readable slugs for note paths and rename files to match titles
rawCommits.body:
Notes now live under <data_dir>/notes/<project-slug>/ instead of the
project UUID, with a `.project-id` marker file binding each folder to
its project so lookups stay stable when the project is renamed and
collisions (`my-project-2`, `my-project-3`) fall back to numeric
suffixes. A one-time migration moves any existing UUID-named folder
into its new slug folder the first time the Notes app opens it.

On every autosave, the handler slugifies the note's first-line title
and renames the `.md` file (plus its `.comments.json` sidecar) to
match, carrying collisions onto a `-2` suffix. The write response now
returns the post-rename `relPath`/`absPath` so the client can rekey
its caches, update the active selection, and quietly rewrite the URL
without disturbing the editor's caret. Also shrink the Notes sidekick
info text to 12px and drop the folder icon from the location link.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Use readable slugs for note paths and rename files to match titles

rawCommits.subject: Auto-select a note on entering the Notes app and stop URL/store loop
rawCommits.body:
Remember the last-opened note in localStorage and, on entering the Notes
app, pick a default: the session-active note, the persisted last note if
it still exists, or the first note found in any project's tree. Drop the
"select a note" empty states from the main panel and sidekick since a
note is always selected.

Also fix an oscillation between the URL and store that produced an HTTP
storm of alternating `/read` + `/comments` requests when switching notes.
Two effects (URL->store and store->URL) were independently "correcting"
the same disagreement in opposite directions, and because zustand and
react-router have separate useSyncExternalStore subscriptions a click
could land on a render where they disagreed. Collapsed them into a
single effect that treats the URL as authoritative and only pushes the
store -> URL when the URL is stable (i.e. an autosave rename has
drifted activeKey under us).

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Auto-select a note on entering the Notes app and stop URL/store loop

rawCommits.subject: Add ZERO product, move New Idea into feed, honor adapter model lists
rawCommits.body:
Feedback:
- Add `zero` to the product whitelist on the server and the matching
  FeedbackProduct enum/filters/options on the client, and relabel
  "The Grid" -> "The GRID" for consistency.
- Lift the New Feedback composer into a modal driven by `isComposerOpen`
  on the store so sidebar/plus and feed-header triggers share one
  instance, and add a "New Idea" button at the top of the feed.
- Track a `hasLoaded` flag so re-entering the Feedback app after the
  first bootstrap doesn't flash the empty/loading state on cached data,
  and make `useFeedbackBootstrap` idempotent across mounts.

ChatInputBar:
- Use the adapter-specific model list in chat mode and fall back to the
  mode-filtered list for image/3d generation.
- Hide the model picker entirely when the current mode has no
  selectable models (e.g. 3d), and stop clobbering the chat model when
  switching to a mode that offers none.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Patch notes tree in place on autosave rename
rawCommits.body:
After an autosave-driven rename, the store was kicking off a full
loadTree round-trip. Between the state update (which had already moved
activeRelPath to the new slug) and the tree refresh, the tree still
held the old node id, so the left-menu selection briefly lost its
highlight and the sidekick Info row visibly flashed. We already have
all the info we need from the write response, so rename the affected
note node synchronously inside the same set() that rekeys
contentCache/commentsByNote and drop the loadTree call.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Patch notes tree in place on autosave rename

rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Pin note title to fixed h1 styling regardless of block type

rawCommits.subject: Add right-click context menu to notes left panel
rawCommits.body:
Mirror the Projects explorer UX in the Notes navigator: right-click (or
F2) on a note or folder now opens a context menu with rename, delete,
reveal-in-folder, and new-note/new-folder actions, backed by its own
modal harness. Right-click on a project row reuses the existing project
context menu via ProjectListModals. The id parsing helpers and hover-
plus glue moved out of NotesNav.tsx into dedicated files so NotesNav
stays a thin composer.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Add right-click context menu to notes left panel

rawCommits.subject: Show production build version in Settings
rawCommits.body:
Bake the version, short commit SHA, build timestamp, and release channel
into the interface bundle at build time and display them in the Settings
view so users can tell exactly which production build they are running.

CI workflows (stable, nightly, mobile nightly, android, iOS) now pass
APP_VERSION / APP_COMMIT / APP_CHANNEL to `npm run build` so the values
shipped in production reflect the deployed release.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Show production build version in Settings

rawCommits.subject: Make super-agent context reset deterministic via cancellable runs
rawCommits.body:
`reset_agent_session` previously cleared only the in-memory conversation
cache; the `tokio::spawn` running the super-agent's multi-turn loop was
untracked, so it kept streaming from the router and, on completion, wrote
its accumulated history back on top of the freshly-cleared cache. The
next user turn then sent the stale conversation into the proxy.

Track each run in a `super_agent_runs` registry with a `CancellationToken`
and a monotonic generation. Reset and `new_session=true` cancel the
token (dropping the `/v1/messages` stream mid-chunk) and bump the
generation; the spawn's cache write is gated on the generation still
matching, so any race past cancellation is discarded.

`SuperAgentStream::run` now returns `SuperAgentRunOutcome::{Completed,
Cancelled}` and short-circuits on a cancelled token both before each
turn and inside `process_stream_response` via `tokio::select!`.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Make super-agent context reset deterministic via cancellable runs

rawCommits.subject: Make the app shell route-driven to fix Notes to Feedback flicker
rawCommits.body:
The shell previously mirrored the active app into `useAppStore.activeApp`
via an effect, so after `navigate('/feedback')` there was one render where
the URL was `/feedback` but the store still pointed at Notes. That stale
render let `NotesMainPanel`'s auto-select effect fire a replace-navigate
back into `/notes/...`, cancelling the app switch.

- Add `useActiveApp` which derives the active app synchronously from
  `useLocation().pathname`. All shell chrome (DesktopShell, MobileShell,
  BottomTaskbar, AppNavRail, sidebar search, project list) now reads from
  it, removing the one-render lag.
- Drop `activeApp` state and `syncActiveApp` from the store. The pathname
  is the single source of truth; `preloadAppForPathname` handles the
  lazy-import warmup side-effect, and TasksProvider takes over the
  "default to tasks sidekick tab" effect it used to piggy-back on.
- Give each app its own `routes.tsx` wired through a new `routes` field on
  `AuraApp`. `App.tsx` flattens the registry's routes under the shell
  layout, making each app module the single source of truth for the URLs
  it handles. `AuraAppModule` is the lazy-loaded panel shape (without
  routing), so heavy app bundles still don't evaluate on cold start.
- Extract Notes auto-select into `NotesIndexRedirect`, mounted only on
  `/notes` and `/notes/:projectId`. It unmounts the instant the user
  navigates to another app, so it can no longer hijack outgoing routes.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Make the app shell route-driven to fix Notes to Feedback flicker

rawCommits.subject: Show build version in Team Settings general section
rawCommits.body:
Users expected the version/commit/build time that used to live in the
standalone SettingsView to be visible in the Team Settings panel. Add
an About group to OrgSettingsGeneral sourced from getBuildInfo().

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Show build version in Team Settings general section

rawCommits.subject: Make session rows non-navigating in agent lists
rawCommits.body:
Clicking a session in the sidekick SessionList, the Agent Info
ChatsTab, and the project AgentStatusBar dropdown no longer
navigates to `?session=...` or opens a session preview. Session
rows still render status, summaries, and context menu actions;
they're just informational displays now.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Make session rows non-navigating in agent lists

rawCommits.subject: Report real super-agent context utilization in AssistantMessageEnd
rawCommits.body:
SuperAgentStream previously hard-coded estimated_context_tokens=0 and
context_utilization=0.0, so the UI's N% context indicator stayed blank
for super-agents even though harness agents populated it via
SessionService.

Capture Anthropic's prompt-side token counts (input + cache_creation +
cache_read) from message_start.usage, track them cumulatively alongside
input/output, and compute context_utilization = prompt_tokens / 200k
(Claude Sonnet/Opus window), clamped to [0, 1]. Populate both the normal
end-of-turn and max_turns AssistantMessageEnd emissions with the real
numbers and cumulative cache totals.

Adds three unit tests covering the fresh+cache math, the >=100% clamp,
and the empty-usage zero case.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Report real super-agent context utilization in AssistantMessageEnd

rawCommits.subject: Refactor Notes and Feedback apps to match React and TypeScript rules
rawCommits.body:
Split monolithic panels into colocated folders so each component owns its
CSS, types, and tests: Notes gets BubbleToolbar, NotesInfoPanel,
NotesCommentsPanel, NotesEntryContextMenu, and NotesEntryModals folders,
while Feedback grows a reusable FeedbackFilterTree wrapper so FeedbackList
is a thin orchestrator. NotesSidekickPanel is now a thin router over the
two new panels, and FeedbackItemCard is rebuilt as an <article> with real
<button> children so vote, comment, and select targets are all keyboard
and screen-reader reachable.

Tighten typing across both apps: replace type assertions with type guards
and instanceof narrowing (NewFeedbackModal, useNotesContextMenu,
notes-store), add an explicit NotesContextMenuApi return type, centralize
tiptap markdown-storage casting in NotesMainPanel, and wire up the
missing setProductFilter action that FeedbackList already consumed.
Fire-and-forget store calls now attach .catch handlers so rejections no
longer surface as unhandled promise warnings.

Clean up presentation: move static inline styles into CSS modules,
tokenize FeedbackList spacing, delete unused CSS and exports, drop
premature useMemo for constant data, and consolidate Feedback filter
labels so they derive from types.ts instead of being duplicated per
sidebar section. Add colocated tests for the new extracted components.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Refactor Notes and Feedback apps to match React and TypeScript rules

rawCommits.subject: Always show desktop background through top bar and taskbar
rawCommits.body:
The background layer, translucent blur on the top bar, and the shell's
data-desktop-mode attribute were previously gated on the desktop app
being active. Render them unconditionally so the configured background
shows through the top bar, taskbar, and surrounding surfaces in every
app, while keeping sidebar collapse and sidekick visibility tied to the
desktop app as before.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Always show desktop background through top bar and taskbar

rawCommits.subject: Close sidekick preview overlays on Escape
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Close sidekick preview overlays on Escape

rawCommits.subject: Restore opaque content surfaces outside desktop mode
rawCommits.body:
After always rendering the background layer, the middle panel, sidebar,
and sidekick kept showing the wallpaper through them when leaving the
desktop app. Scope data-desktop-mode back to isDesktop so those content
surfaces fall back to var(--color-bg), while the top bar and bottom
taskbar stay translucent in every app (taskbar blur is promoted to the
base .bar rule).

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Restore opaque content surfaces outside desktop mode

rawCommits.subject: Close task and spec sidekick preview on Escape
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Close task and spec sidekick preview on Escape

rawCommits.subject: Colocate tests for the remaining Notes and Feedback components
rawCommits.body:
Every component in the Notes and Feedback apps now ships with a
neighbour *.test.tsx that covers its load-bearing behaviour:

- NotesInfoPanel: parseToc handles frontmatter + code fences, word count
  and location render correctly, and the path button calls
  revealInFolder.
- NotesCommentsPanel: empty state, existing comments, Enter-to-submit
  clearing the draft, and whitespace-only drafts staying put.
- NotesNav: empty onboarding hint, loading suppression, and the nav
  tree once projects are present.
- NotesEntryContextMenu: note vs folder menu items, and that selections
  route through handleMenuAction.
- NotesEntryModals: rename input wiring, delete dialog title + folder
  warning, error alerts, and busy-state button disabling.
- NotesMainPanel: empty-URL fallback, Rich/Markdown tab defaults,
  switching into Markdown mode, and onChange -> updateContent.
- FeedbackList: renders every filter section, and maps the synthetic
  "All Types"/"All Statuses" ids to null store values while writing
  real ids through directly.
- FeedbackMainPanel: loading, error, empty, and populated list states,
  plus the New Idea button opening the composer.
- FeedbackSidekickHeader: renders for the selected item, hides without
  one, and the close button clears the selection.
- FeedbackSidekickPanel: empty-no-selection state, no-comments state,
  existing comments, Enter-to-submit, and send-button disable rules.

Heavy dependencies (ZUI, TipTap, the persist layer in browser-db) are
mocked per-file so the assertions focus on each component's own
decisions rather than on dependency internals. Scoped vitest run passes
17 files / 66 tests with no new typecheck or lint issues.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Colocate tests for the remaining Notes and Feedback components

rawCommits.subject: Stabilize desktop shell background across app switches and boot
rawCommits.body:
Move the opaque middle background onto .desktopContent (a shell-level
element that is never wrapped by ActiveProvider) so switching between
apps with a Provider like Process or Tasks no longer unmounts the
bg-carrying div and flashes the wallpaper through. Drop the redundant
.desktopShell[data-desktop-mode] transparency override; the shell keeps
a var(--color-bg) base at all times and the BackgroundLayer paints the
wallpaper above it when present.

Track a hydrated flag in the desktop background store that is true
immediately for color and none modes and only flips for image mode
after the IDB image load resolves. Gate BackgroundLayer rendering and
the data-desktop-mode attribute on that flag so image-mode boots never
paint a broken url() or briefly expose a see-through shell before the
image data is in hand.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Stabilize desktop shell background across app switches and boot

rawCommits.subject: Trim unused UI scaffolding from process and agent chat views
rawCommits.body:
Drop the unused @tiptap/extension-link dependency, remove the process
main panel empty state placeholder, and stop passing the redundant
session empty message to the project agent chat panel.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Trim unused UI scaffolding from process and agent chat views

rawCommits.cleanSubject: Harden changelog generation and add rubric fixtures

rawCommits.cleanSubject: Relax changelog validator count limits

rendered.title: Feedback and Notes apps ship, chat scroll overhauled, agent reliability hardened
rendered.intro: A dense nightly that delivered two new first-class apps — Feedback and Notes — while simultaneously replacing the chat panel's virtualizer with native CSS scroll anchoring to eliminate the long-standing resize jank. Agent session management got deterministic cancellation and proper context scoping, and the release pipeline gained build-version surfacing across all CI targets.

entries.title: Chat scroll rebuilt and Feedback app launched end-to-end
entries.summary: Four iterative JS-side scroll fixes were superseded by a root-cause rewrite to CSS scroll anchoring, while the Feedback app went from mock data to a fully wired backend with votes, product tags, and session reliability improvements.

items.text: Replaced the TanStack virtualizer with native CSS scroll anchoring in the chat panel, eliminating the frame-lag jank that caused messages to slide and pop during sidekick lane resizes. The virtualizer, per-message ResizeObserver, height cache, and ~533 lines of scroll-correction plumbing were removed; a single targeted useLayoutEffect now handles the only case the browser can't cover — pinning to the bottom as new tokens stream in.

All agent tool outputs (thinking, file ops, commands, specs, tasks, media, status readouts) now render through a unified Block primitive with a fixed-height scrollable body. Content streaming inside a Block no longer causes layout shifts in the surrounding chat lane — it behaves like a rolling tail -f inside the block. Stacked tool-row borders were also collapsed from 2px to a clean 1px divider.

The Feedback app launched with a full backend (list, create, vote, status-update, comment endpoints in Rust) and a live frontend store replacing mock data. Votes and status updates are applied optimistically and reconciled with server aggregates, reverting on failure. A dedicated AURA_NETWORK_FEEDBACK_URL override lets feedback traffic target a local aura-network instance independently of the main network client.

Feedback posts can now be tagged with a product (AURA, The Grid, Wilder World, Z Chain) stored in the existing metadata blob. The left filter panel gained a Product section, and the composer defaults to AURA independently of the active filter — fixing a bug where switching the filter retargeted new posts.

Agent session management received two reliability fixes: session reset now scopes LLM context to the current storage session only (preventing corrupted tool_use blocks from prior sessions re-injecting into the model), and stale active sessions are transitioned to completed on rotation so the sidekick no longer shows a spinning status icon for every historical session.

Project delete now returns a clear 409 with an actionable message (pointing to Archive) instead of an opaque 500 when residual foreign-key references block deletion. Spec deletion similarly blocks with a 409 when tasks still reference the spec, surfacing the task count inline.

entries.title: Notes app introduced with TipTap editor and slug-based file paths
entries.summary: A new Notes app lets users write and organize free-form markdown notes per project, with WYSIWYG editing, auto-renaming files to match titles, and a sidekick showing metadata, table of contents, and local comments.

items.text: Notes are stored as plain markdown files on disk under the data directory, organized by project. The TipTap editor supports both WYSIWYG rich-text and raw markdown modes, with live first-line titles. A sidekick panel surfaces file metadata, a generated table of contents, and local comments stored in a sidecar JSON file.

Note files are now stored under human-readable project slugs instead of UUIDs, with a .project-id marker file keeping lookups stable across renames. On every autosave, the file is renamed to match its first-line title (with collision suffixes), and the client rekeys its caches and updates the URL without disturbing the editor caret.

The Notes app now auto-selects a note on entry — restoring the last-opened note from localStorage, falling back to the first note found — and eliminates the empty 'select a note' state. A URL-store oscillation that caused an HTTP storm of alternating read and comment requests when switching notes was also fixed.

Feedback gained a ZERO product option and a 'New Idea' button at the top of the feed. The chat model picker now uses the adapter-specific model list and hides entirely when the current generation mode has no selectable models.

entries.title: Notes polish, route-driven shell, and super-agent reset hardened
entries.summary: Notes received context menus, autosave-rename tree patching, and a fixed title style, while the app shell was refactored to derive the active app from the URL — eliminating a flicker when switching between Notes and Feedback.

items.text: Right-clicking a note or folder in the Notes navigator now opens a context menu with rename, delete, reveal-in-folder, and new-note/new-folder actions, mirroring the Projects explorer UX.

Autosave-driven renames now patch the notes tree in place synchronously instead of triggering a full loadTree round-trip, eliminating the brief highlight loss and sidekick info flash that occurred between the state update and the tree refresh.

The app shell now derives the active app synchronously from the URL pathname instead of mirroring it through a Zustand effect. This eliminated a one-render lag that allowed Notes' auto-select effect to fire a replace-navigate back into /notes/... when switching to Feedback, cancelling the app switch.

Super-agent session reset is now race-condition-free: each run is tracked with a CancellationToken and a monotonic generation counter. Reset cancels the in-flight /v1/messages stream mid-chunk and gates the cache write on the generation still matching, so any race past cancellation is discarded rather than overwriting the fresh session.

The build version, short commit SHA, build timestamp, and release channel are now baked into the interface bundle at build time and displayed in the Settings view. All CI workflows (stable, nightly, mobile nightly, Android, iOS) pass these values to the build so production releases always show the correct metadata.

entries.title: Shell background, keyboard shortcuts, and super-agent context utilization
entries.summary: The desktop wallpaper now shows through the top bar and taskbar in every app, sidekick previews close on Escape, and super-agents report real context utilization percentages in the UI.

items.text: The configured desktop background now shows through the top bar and bottom taskbar in every app, not just the desktop app. Content surfaces (sidebar, middle panel, sidekick) remain opaque outside desktop mode, and a hydration flag prevents image-mode boots from briefly painting a broken or transparent shell before the image data loads.
Pressing Escape now closes sidekick preview overlays and task/spec sidekick previews, making keyboard-driven workflows faster.

Super-agents now report real context utilization to the UI. Anthropic prompt-side token counts (input, cache creation, cache read) are captured from message_start.usage, accumulated cumulatively, and expressed as a percentage of the 200k Claude context window — fixing the blank N% indicator that previously showed for super-agents.
Build version information is now also visible in the Team Settings general section, so operators can confirm the deployed build without navigating to the standalone Settings view.

entries.title: Test coverage locked in for Notes and Feedback
entries.summary: Every component in the Notes and Feedback apps now ships with a colocated test covering its load-bearing behavior, and the desktop shell background was stabilized against app-switch flashes.

items.text: 66 new tests across 17 files cover the full Notes and Feedback component surfaces — editor modes, context menus, rename/delete modals, comment submission, filter trees, sidekick panels, and loading/error/empty states. Heavy dependencies (ZUI, TipTap, browser-db) are mocked per-file so assertions focus on each component's own decisions.

The desktop shell background no longer flashes the wallpaper through when switching between apps that use an ActiveProvider wrapper (e.g. Process, Tasks). The opaque middle background was moved to a shell-level element that is never unmounted during app switches.

entries.title: Release Infrastructure: changelog generation hardened
entries.summary: The daily changelog generation script was significantly strengthened with rubric-based validation fixtures and relaxed count limits to improve output quality checks.

items.text: The changelog generation script was overhauled with rubric fixtures (a known-good candidate and a known-bad generic candidate) and an automated test suite, making it easier to catch regressions in output quality. Validator count limits were also relaxed to reduce false rejections on legitimate multi-entry changelogs.

highlights: Chat scroll jank eliminated via CSS anchor rewrite
Feedback app launched with live API, votes, and product filtering
Notes app added with TipTap editor, slug paths, and context menus
Agent session reset made race-condition-free with cancellable runs
Build version now visible in Settings and Team Settings on all platforms
Release changelog tooling hardened with rubric fixtures
